ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the customer */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "acquisition_months" (
  "month_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each month */,
  "month_name" TEXT /* Name of the month (e.g., March, February) */,
  "month_number" INTEGER /* Number of the month (e.g., 1, 2, 3, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("month_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "acquisition_years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Name of the year (e.g., 2023) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing channel (e.g., Google, Twitter, Yahoo) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marketing channel */,
  "channel_type" TEXT /* Type of marketing channel (e.g., search, social, email) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing channel used for the campaign */,
  "objective" TEXT /* Objective of the campaign (e.g., awareness, conversion) */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign (e.g., age, location, interests) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaigns_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES marketing_channels ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_acquisition" (
  "acquisition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer acquisition event */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer acquired */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign that led to the acquisition */,
  "acquisition_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer was acquired */,
  "month_id" INTEGER /* ID of the month the customer was acquired in */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the year the customer was acquired in */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing channel that led to the acquisition */,
  "acquisition_cost" REAL /* Cost of acquiring the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("acquisition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_acquisition_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES marketing_channels ("channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_acquisition_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_acquisition_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES acquisition_years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_acquisition_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_acquisition_month_id FOREIGN KEY ("month_id") REFERENCES acquisition_months ("month_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user engagement event */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer engaged */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign related to the engagement */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., email open, click, purchase) */,
  "engagement_date" TEXT /* Date of the engagement */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the engagement */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for engagement (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "browser" TEXT /* Browser used for engagement (e.g., Chrome, Firefox) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of impressions */,
  "clicks" INTEGER /* Number of clicks */,
  "conversions" INTEGER /* Number of conversions */,
  "cost" REAL /* Total cost of the campaign */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue generated by the campaign */,
  "roi" REAL /* Return on investment (ROI) of the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_performance_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the customer segment */,
  "segment_type" TEXT /* Type of customer segment (e.g., demographic, behavioral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segment_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment mapping */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer segment */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer was added to the segment */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer was removed from the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segment_mapping_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segment_mapping_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer segment */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the segment was targeted by the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the segment was no longer targeted by the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id", "campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., marketer, analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the platform was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., customer, campaign, report) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, in-app) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "integrations" (
  "integration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each integration */,
  "integration_name" TEXT /* Name of the integration */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key for the integration */,
  "integration_type" TEXT /* Type of integration (e.g., social media, email marketing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("integration_id")
)
